text: What does HTML stand for?
explanation: HTML stands for HyperText Markup Language, the standard language for creating web pages.

text: What tag is used to create a hyperlink in HTML?
explanation: The <a> (anchor) tag is used to create hyperlinks.

text: Which tag defines the largest heading in HTML?
explanation: <h1> defines the largest/most important heading.

text: What is the correct HTML element for inserting a line break?
explanation: is the self-closing tag used to insert a line break.

text: Which attribute specifies alternate text for an image?
explanation: The alt attribute provides alternative text for accessibility.

text: Which HTML element is used to define important (bold + semantic) text?
explanation: <strong> gives text semantic importance.

text: What tag is used to create an ordered list?
explanation: <ol> creates an ordered (numbered) list.

text: What tag is used to create an unordered list?
explanation: <ul> creates an unordered (bulleted) list.

text: Which tag is used for a table row?
explanation: <tr> defines a table row.

text: Which tag defines a table header cell?
explanation: <th> defines a header cell in a table.

text: What tag creates a form in HTML?
explanation: <form> is the container element for all form controls.

text: Which input type attribute value creates a checkbox?
explanation: type="checkbox" renders a checkbox input.

text: Which HTML element is used to embed a JavaScript file?
explanation: <script> embeds or references JavaScript.

text: What is the purpose of the <head> element?
options: Contains metadata and links to external resources
explanation: <head> holds metadata: title, charset, links to CSS/JS.

text: Which attribute opens a hyperlink in a new browser tab?
explanation: target="_blank" opens the link in a new tab or window.

text: How do you write a comment in HTML?
explanation: HTML comments use <!-- comment --> syntax.

text: What is the purpose of the <!DOCTYPE html> declaration?
options: Tells the browser the document is HTML5
explanation: <!DOCTYPE html> instructs browsers to render in HTML5 mode.

text: Which element groups block-level content with no semantic meaning?
explanation: <div> is a generic block-level container.

text: Which element groups inline content with no semantic meaning?
explanation: <span> is a generic inline container.

text: Which attribute makes an HTML input field mandatory before form submission?
explanation: The required attribute prevents form submission if the field is empty.

text: What does CSS stand for?
explanation: CSS stands for Cascading Style Sheets.

text: How do you select an element with id 'header' in CSS?
explanation: The # symbol targets an element by its id.

text: How do you select all elements with class 'btn' in CSS?
explanation: A period (.) selects all elements with that class.

text: Which CSS property changes the text color of an element?
explanation: The color property sets the text/foreground color.

text: Which CSS property changes the background color?
explanation: background-color sets the background color.

text: Which CSS property makes text bold?
explanation: font-weight: bold renders text in bold.

text: Which CSS property controls spacing INSIDE an element's border?
explanation: padding adds space between content and border.

text: Which CSS property controls spacing OUTSIDE an element's border?
explanation: margin adds space outside the element's border.

text: How do you apply the same style to multiple CSS selectors at once?
options: Separate them with a semicolon: h1; h2 { }
Separate them with a comma: h1, h2 { }
explanation: Comma-separated selectors share the same rule block.

text: How do you make an element a flex container?
explanation: display: flex activates flexbox on a container.

text: Which CSS property rounds the corners of an element?
explanation: border-radius rounds the corners of an element.

text: Which CSS property adds a shadow to a box/element?
explanation: box-shadow adds shadows around an element.

text: How do you import a Google Font in CSS?
explanation: @import url('...') loads an external font.

text: Which CSS property sets the transparency of an element?
explanation: opacity accepts values 0 (transparent) to 1 (opaque).

text: How do you create a CSS grid layout?
explanation: display: grid activates CSS Grid on a container.

text: What are the layers of the CSS box model from inside out?
explanation: The CSS box model: Content → Padding → Border → Margin.

text: How do you horizontally center a block element in CSS?
explanation: margin: 0 auto centers a block element with a defined width.

text: What is the difference between display: block and display: inline?
options: Block adds bold text; inline adds italic text
Block takes full width and starts on a new line; inline flows within text
Block is for divs only; inline is for spans only
explanation: Block elements take full width on a new line. Inline elements flow within text.

text: What does the z-index property control?
options: The zoom level of an element
The horizontal position of an element
The stacking order of positioned elements
explanation: z-index controls the stacking order. Higher values appear on top.

text: What does overflow: hidden do?
options: Clips any content that exceeds the element's box
explanation: overflow: hidden clips content beyond the element's boundaries.

text: What does JavaScript primarily do on the web?
options: Adds interactivity and dynamic behaviour to web pages
explanation: JavaScript makes pages interactive by manipulating the DOM and handling events.

text: How do you declare a variable that cannot be reassigned in JavaScript?
explanation: const declares a block-scoped variable that cannot be reassigned.

text: How do you write a single-line comment in JavaScript?
explanation: // starts a single-line comment.

text: What does console.log() do in JavaScript?
options: Writes output to the HTML page
Prints a value to the browser's developer console
explanation: console.log() outputs values to the DevTools console.

text: How do you select an element by its id in JavaScript?
explanation: document.getElementById('id') returns the matching DOM element.

text: What does typeof return when used on a number in JavaScript?
explanation: typeof returns 'number' for any numeric value.

text: How do you create an array in JavaScript?
explanation: Arrays are created with square brackets [].

text: How do you add an item to the END of an array in JavaScript?
explanation: arr.push(item) appends items to the end of an array.

text: What does the array.length property return?
options: The index of the last element
The size in bytes of the array
The number of elements in the array
The highest numeric value in the array
explanation: array.length returns the count of elements.

text: Which modern method loops over every element in an array?
explanation: arr.forEach(callback) calls the callback for each element.

text: What is an arrow function in JavaScript?
options: A function that returns an object
A shorthand function syntax: const fn = (params) => expression
A function that only works in arrays
A function declared with the arrow keyword
explanation: Arrow functions are a concise syntax that also do not bind their own 'this'.

text: What does the return statement do in a JavaScript function?
options: Repeats the function from the beginning
Throws an error and stops the function
Exits the function and sends a value back to the caller
Logs the value to the console
explanation: return stops function execution and sends the value back to the caller.

text: What is an object literal in JavaScript?
options: A collection of key-value pairs: const obj = { key: value }
explanation: An object literal uses curly braces to define key-value pairs.

text: How do you access the property 'name' of an object called 'user'?
explanation: Dot notation (user.name) and bracket notation (user['name']) both work.

text: What does JSON.stringify() do?
options: Parses a JSON string into an object
Converts a JavaScript object to a JSON string
explanation: JSON.stringify(obj) converts a value into a JSON-formatted string.

text: What does JSON.parse() do?
options: Converts an object to a string
Validates whether a string is valid JSON
Converts a JSON string into a JavaScript object
explanation: JSON.parse(str) converts a JSON string back into a JavaScript object.

text: What is the DOM in web development?
options: A database for storing web data
Document Object Model — a tree representation of the HTML page in memory
explanation: The DOM is a live tree structure browsers create from HTML.

text: How do you add a click event listener to an element in JavaScript?
explanation: addEventListener is the modern way to attach events.

text: How do you convert a string to a number in JavaScript?
explanation: Number('42'), parseInt('42'), or +'42' all return 42.

text: What does NaN mean in JavaScript?
options: Null and Null — double null value
Not a Number — result of an invalid numeric operation
New and New — a duplicate object
Nested and Nested — a deep object
explanation: NaN (Not a Number) is returned when a math operation fails.
